Index.title: Edu Stack IS
Index.description: Information system for supporting informatics education.

hero.title: Edu Stack IS
hero.subtitle: Discover the brain of the school through simulation.
hero.description: Edu Stack IS is an educational tool designed to help students understand the complexity of school management, data flows, and security in the digital age.

idea.title: The Idea
idea.description: Shifting the perception of information systems from passive tools to active educational instruments.
idea.point2: Simulate real school processes (registry, grading, schedule).
idea.point3: Analyze systemic impacts of administrative decisions.

tech.title: Technical Description
tech.description: Modern, scalable, and globally distributed infrastructure.
tech.stack: Built with NestJS, React, Cloudflare D1, and MCP for AI integration.
tech.architecture: Modular monorepo architecture with serverless edge computing.

rules.title: Rules & Licensing
rules.description: Edu Stack IS is an open-source project.
rules.clause1: Available on GitHub for review and non-commercial educational use.
rules.clause2: Commercial usage requires a specific agreement.

Worksheets.title: Working Sheets
Worksheets.description: Detailed scenarios and methodological guides extracted from the bachelor thesis.

primary.title: Primary School (4th-5th Grade)
primary.lesson1: Explorer and the Mystery of Roles
primary.lesson2: A Day in the Teacher's Role
primary.lesson3: The Director and the Whole School

Manual.title: User Manual
Manual.intro: Complete guide to setting up and operating the Edu Stack Information System.

development.title: Local Development
development.description: How to set up the development environment on your local machine.
development.step2: Install dependencies using `npm install` in the root directory.
development.step3: Configure environment variables in `.env` files for both frontend and backend.
development.step4: Start the development server with `npm run dev`.

setup.title: System Setup
setup.description: Initializing the system for the first time.

option1.title: Seed Data (Recommended)
option1.description: Quickly populate the system with demo schools, users, and schedules using the `npm run db:seed` command.

option2.title: From Scratch
option2.description: Start with an empty database and create your first System Administrator through the CLI.

helper.title: Login Helper
helper.description: A developer tool on the login screen to quickly switch between demo identities without passwords.

credentials.title: Email & Password
credentials.description: Standard secure authentication using hashed passwords in the D1 database.

sso.title: SSO Integration
sso.description: Enterprise-grade login support for Google, GitHub, and Microsoft accounts.

systemAdmin.title: System Administrator
systemAdmin.description: Global orchestrator of the entire platform. Handles infrastructure and multi-tenant management.
systemAdmin.functions: School lifecycle management (create/suspend/delete), Global System Admin management, SSO provider configuration (Google, GitHub, MS), AI API key management (Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic), Global AI usage monitoring and token analytics.

workflow1.title: School Management
workflow1.description: Create new school tenants and manage their active/suspended status.
workflow1.step1: Navigate to the 'Schools' module in the System Sidebar.
workflow1.step2: Click 'Create New School' and fill in the school name and unique slug.
workflow1.step3: Assign at least one initial School Administrator.
workflow1.step4: Save and verify the school appears in the global list.

workflow2.title: Identity Providers
workflow2.description: Configure OAuth2/OpenID Connect settings for SSO.
workflow2.step2: Select a provider (Google, GitHub, or Microsoft).
workflow2.step3: Enter Client ID, Client Secret, and Callback URL.
workflow2.step4: Enable the provider and test the login flow.

workflow3.title: AI Resource Orchestration
workflow3.description: Manage API keys and monitor token consumption.
workflow3.step2: Input API keys for Google Gemini or OpenAI.
workflow3.step3: Define global token limits per school to prevent cost overruns.
workflow3.step4: Review the 'AI Usage' dashboard for real-time analytics.

schoolAdmin.title: School Administrator
schoolAdmin.description: Technical manager of a specific school instance.
schoolAdmin.functions: School registry management, User invitation and role assignments, CSV user import/export, Audit log monitoring, School-wide settings and module configuration, User impersonation for support.

workflow1.title: Bulk User Onboarding
workflow1.description: Import entire classes or staff lists using CSV.
workflow1.step2: Download the CSV template and populate it with user data.
workflow1.step3: Upload the file and map the columns to system fields.
workflow1.step4: Verify the import and trigger automated invitation emails.

workflow2.title: Account Impersonation
workflow2.description: Securely log into user accounts for support.
workflow2.step1: Find the target user in the 'Users' table.
workflow2.step2: Click the 'Impersonate' button (available for management roles).
workflow2.step3: The system will reload with the user's context, marked by a top banner.
workflow2.step4: Fix the issue and click 'Leave Impersonation' to return.

workflow3.title: System Audit
workflow3.description: Review detailed logs of all critical actions.
workflow3.step2: Filter by date, user, or action type (e.g., 'Grade Deleted').
workflow3.step3: Expand a log entry to see old and new values side-by-side.
workflow3.step4: Export the report for school compliance records.

headmaster.title: Headmaster
headmaster.description: Pedagogical and organizational leader of the school.
headmaster.functions: Access to high-level school dashboard and statistics, School-wide event planning, Public community announcements and broadcasts, Monitoring of educational quality and teacher performance.

workflow1.title: Strategic Oversight
workflow1.description: Monitor real-time statistics and school-wide KPI.
workflow1.step1: View the 'Principal Dashboard' for high-level summaries.
workflow1.step2: Analyze grade distributions across different departments.
workflow1.step3: Check attendance percentages for the current semester.
workflow1.step4: Identify classes or subjects with abnormal patterns for review.

workflow2.title: School Communications
workflow2.description: Post official announcements and manage bulletins.
workflow2.step2: Click 'New Announcement' and select the target audience (All, Staff, or Students).
workflow2.step3: Write the content and pin critical messages to the top.

workflow3.title: Academic Calendar
workflow3.description: Oversee and approve the yearly plan.
workflow3.step2: Add key milestones like holidays, exams, or meetings.
workflow3.step3: Set visibility permissions for each event.
workflow3.step4: Sync with the public school dashboard for parents and students.

deputy.title: Deputy Principal
deputy.description: Operational manager focusing on the technical organization.
deputy.functions: Academic year and semester setup, Classroom and building management, Subject and curriculum (SVP) design, Complex schedule planning and collision detection, Substitution management, Class registry oversight.

workflow1.title: Interactive Schedule Planning
workflow1.description: Plan the timetable with collision detection.
workflow1.step2: Select a class and drag subjects into available time slots.
workflow1.step3: The system will flag red markers for teacher or room collisions.
workflow1.step4: Resolve collisions and publish the final version for the school.

workflow2.title: Substitution Management
workflow2.step1: Check the 'Absence List' for teacher notifications.
workflow2.step2: Go to 'Substitutions' and select the affected lessons.
workflow2.step3: Assign a substitute teacher or change the room/subject.
workflow2.step4: Save to instantly update the affected users' personal schedules.

workflow3.title: Curriculum Design (SVP)
workflow3.step2: Create subject templates and define hours per week per grade.
workflow3.step4: Activate the curriculum version for the upcoming academic year.

teacher.title: Teacher
teacher.description: The educational core of the system.
teacher.functions: Grade book management (marks, categories, weights), Attendance tracking and excuse management, Class book entries and signing, Thematic plans and lesson preparations, AI Teaching Assistant (lesson generation, report card polishing).

workflow1.title: Grade Book Management
workflow1.description: Enter grades and manage weighted averages.
workflow1.step1: Select a subject from your 'Teaching Overview'.
workflow1.step2: Choose 'Add Grade' and define the category (e.g., 'Big Test').
workflow1.step3: Input grades for the students and add optional comments.
workflow1.step4: Verify that the weighted average is calculated automatically.

workflow2.title: Digital Class Book
workflow2.step1: Open the 'Class Book' during your active lesson.
workflow2.step3: Record the lesson topic according to the thematic plan.
workflow2.step4: Digitally sign the entry to finalize the record.

workflow3.title: AI Pedagogical Assistant
workflow3.description: Use AI to generate plans and polish reports.
workflow3.step1: Open the 'AI Assistant' from the lesson preparation page.
workflow3.step2: Provide a topic and target grade for the lesson plan.
workflow3.step3: Review the generated structure and export to your preparations.
workflow3.step4: Use AI to improve the language of verbal report card evaluations.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: The guardian overseeing the educational journey of their children.
parent.functions: Monitoring grades and academic progress, Electronic absence excuse submission, Communication with teachers and school management, Viewing child's personalized schedule and substitutions.

workflow1.title: Academic Oversight
workflow1.description: Stay informed about your child's performance.
workflow1.step1: Select your child's profile from the dashboard switcher.
workflow1.step2: Open the 'Grades' module to see all recent assessments.
workflow1.step3: Review weighted averages and teacher comments for each subject.
workflow1.step4: Set up email notifications for new grade entries.

workflow2.title: Absence Management
workflow2.description: Officially excuse your child's missed lessons.
workflow2.step2: Click 'New Excuse' and select the date range.
workflow2.step3: Provide the reason for the absence (e.g., 'Medical').
workflow2.step4: Submit the request and monitor its approval status by the teacher.

workflow3.title: Direct Communication
workflow3.description: Engage with the school community effectively.
workflow3.step1: Use the 'Messages' module to start a thread with a teacher.
workflow3.step2: Participate in school polls and respond to event invitations (RSVP).
workflow3.step3: Read pinned announcements on the school bulletin board.
workflow3.step4: Check the schedule for upcoming Parent-Teacher meetings.

student.title: Student
student.description: The primary user of educational services.
student.functions: Personal dashboard with grade overview, Interactive daily and weekly schedule, Community messaging and polls, Access to teaching materials, AI Study Partner for educational support.

workflow1.title: Personal Progress Tracking
workflow1.step1: Check the 'Dashboard' for recent grades and announcements.
workflow1.step2: Open the 'Grades' module to see a subject-by-subject view.
workflow1.step3: Click on any grade to read specific teacher comments.
workflow1.step4: Monitor your overall average and predicted final grade.

workflow2.title: Interactive Timetable
workflow2.description: Access your schedule and substitution info.
workflow2.step2: Toggle between Daily and Weekly views.
workflow2.step3: Substitution changes will be highlighted in orange.
workflow2.step4: Click on a lesson to see the assigned room and teacher.

workflow3.title: AI Study Partner
workflow3.description: Get help with concepts and summaries.
workflow3.step1: Open the 'AI Study Partner' chat drawer.
workflow3.step2: Ask a question about your current subject topics.
workflow3.step3: The AI uses school context to provide relevant answers.
workflow3.step4: Request summaries of teaching materials or practice quiz questions.

Footer.description: Modern information system designed to transform informatics education through interactive simulation.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: At Edu Stack IS, we take your privacy seriously. This presentation website is purely informational.

section1.title: Data Collection
section1.content: We do not collect any personal data from visitors of this presentation website. No cookies are used for tracking or profiling.

section2.title: Information System Data
section2.content: The Edu Stack IS application itself handles data according to the configurations set by the hosting educational institution. For the sandbox version, data is handled according to our non-commercial open-source rules.

section3.title: Third-Party Services
section3.content: This site is hosted on Cloudflare Pages. Cloudflare may collect standard web server logs for security and performance purposes.

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.intro: By accessing this website, you agree to the following terms.

section1.title: Usage of Content
section1.content: The content on this website is for educational and presentation purposes. You may use the information here to understand the Edu Stack IS project.

section2.title: Software License
section2.content: The Edu Stack IS software is open-source. Usage is restricted to non-commercial educational purposes. Any commercial usage requires a written agreement with the author.

section3.title: Disclaimer
section3.content: The information provided here is 'as is' without any warranties. We are not responsible for any issues arising from the use of the information or software presented here.
